methods.description: Collection of Triturus carnifex individuals from various localities across the species' range.

steps.description: A total of 231 Triturus carnifex individuals were sampled from 37 localities spanning the species' range.
steps.searchable_summary: collect biological samples from specified geographic locations

steps.description: Detailed information about sampling localities and number of individuals sampled in each locality are shown in Table 1 and Figure 1C.
steps.searchable_summary: record metadata for collected samples including location and quantity

methods.description: Extraction of genomic DNA from collected Triturus carnifex samples.

steps.description: DNA extraction was performed by following the standard cetyltrimethyl ammonium bromide (CTAB) protocol [47].
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA from biological samples using CTAB protocol

methods.description: Amplification of mitochondrial DNA fragments (ND4 and ND2) using Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR).

steps.description: Prepare PCR reaction mix in a 25 ml volume containing MgCl2 (2.5 mM), reaction buffer (56, Promega), the four dNTPs (0.2 mM each), the two primers (0.2 mM each), the enzyme Taq polymerase (1 U, Promega) and 2 ml of DNA template.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare PCR reaction mixture with specified reagents and concentrations

steps.description: Preliminary amplifications and sequencing of the ND4 fragment were performed using primers ND4 and LEU [48], and then the internal primers ND4carnF1 (ACCCCATTAACAAAAGAAATAGCA) and ND4carnR2 (GTGTTTCATAACTCTTCTTGGTGTG) were designed and used to screen all individuals.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR amplification for ND4 gene using specified primers

steps.description: Preliminary amplifications and sequencing of the ND2 fragment were performed using primers H5018 and L3780 [49], and then the internal primer TCND2F2 (TCCTTGCTTGAATAGGACTAGAAAT) was designed and used in conjunction with H5018 to screen all individuals.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR amplification for ND2 gene using specified primers

steps.description: Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) was performed with a step at 95uC for 5 min followed by 30 (ND4) or 35 (ND2) cycles of: 94uC for 1 min, 56uC for 1 min, 72uC for 1 min, and a single final step at 72uC for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: execute thermal cycling profile for PCR amplification

methods.description: Purification of amplified PCR products and subsequent sequencing to obtain DNA sequences.

steps.description: Purification and sequencing of the PCR products were carried out by Macrogen Inc. (www.macrogen. com) by using an ABI PRISM 3700 sequencing system.
steps.searchable_summary: purify PCR products and perform DNA sequencing

steps.description: All sequences were deposited in GenBank (accession numbers: JQ598071-JQ598166).
steps.searchable_summary: deposit DNA sequences into public database (GenBank)

methods.description: Initial processing of raw DNA sequence data including checking and alignment.

steps.description: Electropherograms were visually checked using FinchTv 1.4.0 [50]
steps.searchable_summary: visually inspect raw sequence electropherograms for quality

steps.description: and aligned using Clustal 62.0 [51].
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple sequence alignment

methods.description: Analysis of genetic variation within the aligned sequence dataset.

steps.description: MEGA5 [52] was used to analyse sequence variation.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sequence variation metrics (e.g., nucleotide diversity, haplotype diversity)

methods.description: Selection of the best-fit nucleotide substitution model for subsequent phylogenetic analyses.

steps.description: The best-fit model of nucleotide substitution for our dataset was selected among 88 alternative models using the Akaike Information Criterion (AIC, [53]) implemented in jModelTest 0.1.1 [54].
steps.searchable_summary: select best-fit nucleotide substitution model using AIC

steps.description: We first analysed the ND4 and ND2 fragments separately, and then combined.
steps.searchable_summary: perform model selection on individual gene fragments and combined dataset

steps.description: TIM1+C [55] was the best fit model in all cases. Consequently, the combined dataset (with the gamma distribution shape parameter = 0.10) was used in all subsequent analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and record the selected best-fit model and parameters

methods.description: Inferring phylogenetic relationships among haplotypes using the Maximum Likelihood (ML) method.

steps.description: ML analyses were performed with PhyML 3.0 [56].
steps.searchable_summary: perform Maximum Likelihood phylogenetic tree inference

steps.description: Use the closely related species T. macedonicus was used as outgroup (GenBank accession number NC015794).
steps.searchable_summary: specify outgroup sequence for phylogenetic analysis

steps.description: Tree topologies were estimated using the SPR&NNI option, which performs both the available methods (i.e the Nearest Neighbor Interchanges (NNI), and the Subtree Pruning and Regrafting (SPR)) and returns the best solution among the two.
steps.searchable_summary: optimize tree topology using SPR and NNI algorithms

steps.description: The robustness of the inferred ML and MP tree topologies was assessed by the nonparametric bootstrap method with 1000 replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: assess phylogenetic tree branch support using nonparametric bootstrap
outputs: phylogenetic tree with bootstrap support values

methods.description: Inferring phylogenetic relationships among haplotypes using the Maximum Parsimony (MP) method.

steps.description: MP analysis was computed using PAUP [57], with all characters equally weighted and unordered.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Maximum Parsimony phylogenetic tree inference

steps.description: A heuristic search was carried out, with tree bisection and reconnection (TBR) branch swapping and 10 rounds of random sequence addition.
steps.searchable_summary: perform heuristic tree search using TBR branch swapping and random sequence addition

steps.description: The robustness of the inferred ML and MP tree topologies was assessed by the nonparametric bootstrap method with 1000 replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: assess phylogenetic tree branch support using nonparametric bootstrap
outputs: phylogenetic tree with bootstrap support values

methods.description: Inferring phylogenetic relationships among haplotypes using a statistical parsimony network approach.

steps.description: Phylogenetic relationships among haplotypes were also inferred by the statistical parsimony procedure for phylogenetic network estimations [58] by using the software TCS 1.2.1 [59].
steps.searchable_summary: infer phylogenetic network using statistical parsimony

methods.method_name: Time to Most Recent Common Ancestor (TMRCA) Estimation
methods.description: Estimating divergence times for main mtDNA lineages using distance-based methods.

steps.description: The hypothesis of clock-like evolution of our sequences was assessed by performing a likelihood ratio test in DAMBE.
steps.searchable_summary: test for molecular clock hypothesis using likelihood ratio test

steps.description: To specify a tree topology we used the ML tree previously estimated by PhyML.
steps.searchable_summary: input a specified tree topology for TMRCA estimation
outputs: specified tree topology for TMRCA analysis

steps.description: The divergence between T. carnifex and T. macedonicus was used to set a calibration point. According to Arntzen et al. [62], this divergence was estimated to date back to the end of the Messinian salinity crisis and the consequent reflooding of the Adriatic Sea (5.337 million years ago (Ma)).
steps.searchable_summary: set a calibration point for molecular dating based on known divergence time
inputs: lineages for calibration (T. carnifex, T. macedonicus)

steps.description: Time to the most recent common ancestor (TMRCA) of the main mtDNA lineages was estimated by using the distance-based least squares (LS) methods recently described by Xia & Yang [60] and implemented in the software DAMBE [61].
steps.searchable_summary: estimate TMRCA using distance-based least squares method

steps.description: Finally, to perform the LS analysis in DAMBE we set the 'softbound' option and 'MLCompositeTN93' genetic distance, as suggested by Xia & Yang [60], along with 1000 bootstrap re-samplings to obtain standard deviations of the time estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: configure and run LS analysis with specific settings and bootstrap resampling

methods.description: Analyzing the hierarchical distribution of genetic variance among groups, populations within groups, and within populations.

steps.description: To understand how genetic variance was hierarchically distributed among groups, among populations within groups, and within populations, we performed the analysis of molecular variance (AMOVA) by using Arlequin 3.5.1.2 [63].
steps.searchable_summary: perform Analysis of Molecular Variance (AMOVA)

steps.description: Groups were defined a priori, according to the main geographic discontinuities in the distribution of genetic variation, as defined by previous phylogenetic analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: define hierarchical groups for AMOVA based on prior information

steps.description: The analysis was run using the Tamura & Nei model (TrN+C [64]), which is the best approximation of the TIM1+C model available in ARLEQUIN.
steps.searchable_summary: specify genetic distance model for AMOVA

steps.description: The significance of the variance components and fixation indices was tested using 10100 permutations.
steps.searchable_summary: test significance of AMOVA results using permutations

methods.description: Assessing the correlation between geographic and genetic distances separating populations.

steps.description: To assess the occurrence of a significant pattern of isolation-bydistance, the correlation between geographic and genetic distances separating populations was evaluated using Mantel tests with the software ZT [65].
steps.searchable_summary: perform Mantel test to assess correlation between geographic and genetic distances

steps.description: Following suggestions by Rousset [66], geographic distances were log-transformed
steps.searchable_summary: log-transform geographic distance matrix
